original_question: Why is RAG useful for reducing unsupported answers from an LLM?

choices.A: It checks relevant records before writing, so the reply is less likely to rest only on memory.
choices.B: It improves the result only by making the message longer.
choices.C: It works mainly by changing the names of answer choices.
choices.D: It guarantees a correct answer without checking evidence.

primitive_phrase: first searches a store of relevant records before composing a reply, so unsupported statements from an automatic writing system can be reduced

original_question: Why is an mRNA vaccine useful for training the body to recognize a disease marker?

choices.A: It delivers a temporary instruction that causes the body to make a harmless marker for practice.
choices.B: It works by making a message longer before treatment.
choices.C: It succeeds by hiding the disease marker from the body.
choices.D: It guarantees immunity without any biological response.

primitive_phrase: delivers a temporary instruction that causes the body to make a harmless marker for practice

original_question: Why is an API useful when two software services need to cooperate?

choices.A: It removes the need for any shared request format.
choices.B: It lets both systems guess randomly among alternatives.
choices.C: It gives both systems a stable contract for requests and replies.
choices.D: It works mainly by changing the user-facing label.

primitive_phrase: offers a published set of commands one machine can use to request work from another

original_question: How does GPS mainly help a traveler find a route through an unfamiliar city?

choices.A: It estimates location from signals and compares that location with a stored map.
choices.B: It guarantees the road will be empty.
choices.C: It hides the traveler's position from every instrument.
choices.D: It chooses a route by changing the names of streets.

primitive_phrase: uses timed radio signals from known points to estimate position

original_question: Why can a recommendation algorithm help choose an article a person may like?

choices.A: It ranks options using patterns in earlier choices and similarities.
choices.B: It removes every past choice from consideration.
choices.C: It succeeds because it never compares records.
choices.D: It guarantees a correct choice without evidence.

primitive_phrase: ranks options using patterns in earlier choices and similarities

original_question: How does an API help an order system ask a payment system to do work?

choices.A: It gives both systems a stable contract for requests and replies.
choices.B: It improves cooperation only by making the message longer.
choices.C: It works by hiding every command from both machines.
choices.D: It removes the need for any agreed format.

primitive_phrase: offers a published set of commands one machine can use to request work from another
